content:
{
  "title": "Adaptive Meta-Prompting for Robustness Against LLM Jailbreaks",
  "problem": "Large language models remain vulnerable to adversarial prompts that bypass safety guardrails, often by exploiting model misalignment or prompt injection. Existing static safety prompts are insufficiently robust to the diversity of jailbreak attempts.",
  "motivation": "With the growing deployment of LLMs in real-world applications, adversarial misuse poses serious risks. Improving prompt-level robustness is urgent as attackers rapidly iterate new jailbreak techniques, outpacing static defenses.",
  "method": "We propose 'adaptive meta-prompting,' where the initial user prompt is dynamically wrapped in a meta-prompt that combines (1) automated context-sensitive threat detection (via lightweight classifier), (2) on-the-fly synthesis of adversarially-resistant instruction scaffolding, and (3) prompt randomization (e.g., variable phrasing, order) to reduce overfitting to known jailbreak patterns. This meta-prompt is regenerated per interaction, aiming to disrupt both direct and indirect adversarial cues while minimally impacting benign use.",
  "experiment_plan": "Test on AdvBench, JailbreakBench, and benign Alpaca prompts. Baselines: standard LLM; static safety prompt; prior adversarial prompt defenses. Metrics: attack success rate (lower is better), benign utility (BLEU, human eval), and adversarial transferability. Use minimal 100-example splits to enable rapid iteration and falsification."
}
